channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: US allies hit with new tariffs object to Trump's forced labor claims
item.description: Australia and the European Union were among U.S. trade partners who condemned the tariffs.
item.content:encoded:
Australia and the European Union were among U.S. trade partners who condemned the tariffs.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: What animal is the deformed-but-adorable Jimothy? The quiz knows — do you?
item.description: Also: Ryan Gosling's regrets, Zohran Mamdani's exhortations and the Smithsonian's alleged wokeness.
item.content:encoded: Also: Ryan Gosling's regrets, Zohran Mamdani's exhortations and the Smithsonian's alleged wokeness.
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Trump refuses to give up on tariffs - a pillar of his economic legacy
item.description: The administration timed a new set of tariffs on all of its largest trading partners to take effect at the same time the old, global tariff ended.
item.content:encoded:
The administration timed a new set of tariffs on all of its largest trading partners to take effect at the same time the old, global tariff ended.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Netflix viewers the world over are binge-watching South African series 'The Polygamist.' Here's why
item.description: Why is a Zulu-language drama taking the world by storm? The Polygamist mixes sex and scandal with a sharp portrait of modern South Africa.
item.content:encoded:
Why is a Zulu-language drama taking the world by storm? The Polygamist mixes sex and scandal with a sharp portrait of modern South Africa.

(Image credit: Mpumelelo Macu)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: Video: Scientists watch orcas ram a sunfish so hard it explodes
item.description: Marine biologists off of Baja California recorded one orca teeing up another to smash a sunfish into pieces. Scientists say there could be a reason for this behavior — or it could be just for entertainment.
item.content:encoded:
Marine biologists off of Baja California recorded one orca teeing up another to smash a sunfish into pieces. Scientists say there could be a reason for this behavior — or it could be just for entertainment.

(Image credit: NurPhoto)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: West Virginia paid digital nomads to move there during COVID. Many are staying
item.description: Ascend West Virginia is one of dozens of relocation initiatives across the country offering cash and other incentives to move to states needing an economic boost. Here's how they're working out.
item.content:encoded:
Ascend West Virginia is one of dozens of relocation initiatives across the country offering cash and other incentives to move to states needing an economic boost. Here's how they're working out.

(Image credit: Chris Jackson for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S.-Iran strikes escalate, affecting global energy markets, Trump imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries, Vigil held in Madison, Wisconsin, for man killed by police.
item.content:encoded: U.S.-Iran strikes escalate, affecting global energy markets, Trump imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries, Vigil held in Madison, Wisconsin, for man killed by police.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: He was falsely accused in the 1996 Atlanta Olympics bombing. How it changed his life
item.description: When a bomb exploded at the Summer Olympics in Atlanta 30 years ago, security guard Richard Jewell was named the primary suspect. His friend and attorney reflects on how that changed Jewell's life.
item.content:encoded: When a bomb exploded at the Summer Olympics in Atlanta 30 years ago, security guard Richard Jewell was named the primary suspect. His friend and attorney reflects on how that changed Jewell's life.
item.dc:creator: Jasmyn Morris

item.title: Indian activist Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike as 'Cockroach' protesters vow to press on
item.description: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike on Friday that helped galvanize a youth movement that's become one of India's largest protests in recent years, but demonstrators vowed to continue until the education minister resigns.
item.content:encoded:
Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike on Friday that helped galvanize a youth movement that's become one of India's largest protests in recent years, but demonstrators vowed to continue until the education minister resigns.

(Image credit: Vipin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rubio says U.S. is ready to help end war in Ukraine but no quick deal ahead
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who met with his Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov, said that President Trump was committed to the work "if conditions and factors have changed to make that possible."
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who met with his Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov, said that President Trump was committed to the work "if conditions and factors have changed to make that possible."

(Image credit: BRENDAN SMIALOWSKI)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
